behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide
behaviors.justification: This is pure, deterministic computation with no I/O, database, or external dependencies. A unit test calling the key generation function directly will catch non-determinism, collisions, and parameter-ordering bugs. No mock can hide a collision or non-determinism — the function either produces consistent, unique outputs or it does not.
behaviors.test_description: Create test cases with identical query parameters, verify keys match. Create variations (different parameter values, reordered parameters, null values, type variations) and verify each produces unique keys. Test collision resistance with hundreds of parameter combinations.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour)
behaviors.justification: A unit test with mocked config will test computation logic but misses real failures: malformed config files not parsing, category entries missing from production config, wrong configuration file loaded, or incorrect values in the real source. The behavior depends on reading configured freshness rules from a file, database, or external source. Only Integration tests with real (or near-real) config verify that the production configuration is actually loaded and used correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a test configuration with multiple data categories mapped to TTL values (user_profiles → 5 min, product_catalog → 1 hour, etc.). Load config from its real source (file, database, or configuration service). Call the TTL computation function for each category. Verify each returns the expected TTL. Include tests for missing categories (fallback behavior), malformed config, and edge cases like zero or negative TTL values.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration
behaviors.justification: A unit test with mocked memory usage will test only the strategy-selection logic, not the actual behavior. Real failures are missed: memory usage is measured incorrectly, the memory threshold comparison is wrong, or the cache doesn't actually switch algorithms under real memory pressure. The behavior requires actual cache state, real memory measurement, and verification that the strategy change affects subsequent evictions. A mock can always return the same memory value, hiding bugs in measurement or threshold comparison.
behaviors.test_description: Create a cache instance with a configurable, low memory threshold (e.g., 100KB). Populate the cache with entries until actual memory usage exceeds the threshold. Verify the eviction strategy changes from LRU to LFU. Trigger subsequent cache fills and verify that evictions follow the LFU algorithm (removing least-frequently-used entries), not LRU. Test multiple threshold crossings and verify the strategy reverts to LRU if memory pressure decreases.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows
behaviors.justification: A unit test with mocked database and cache misses the actual invalidation mechanism: triggers may be disabled, polling may not run, notifications may not be sent, or row-to-key mapping may be incorrect in the real system. The behavior is only correct if writes to PostgreSQL actually trigger invalidation. Real failures (stale cache after a write, wrong entries invalidated, invalidation mechanism broken) cannot be caught by in-process mocks. Integration with a real test database instance is the minimum to verify the trigger/listener/poller actually works.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a test PostgreSQL instance with a table and populated cache. Write a row to the PostgreSQL table (insert, update, or delete). Verify that all cache entries whose keys correspond to the affected row are invalidated. Verify that unrelated cache entries remain intact. Test batch writes that affect multiple rows and verify all corresponding cache entries are invalidated. Include tests for missing rows (no cached entries to invalidate) and concurrent reads during invalidation.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full
behaviors.justification: This is a testable algorithm with deterministic inputs and outputs. Unit tests can create a cache, add entries with controlled access patterns (known timestamps for LRU, known access counts for LFU), trigger eviction, and verify the correct entry was removed. No I/O, network, or infrastructure is required. The algorithm's correctness is independent of how the cache is used in production; a unit test isolates the eviction logic and directly verifies its behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create cache instances configured for LRU and LFU. For LRU tests: add entries A, B, C; access them in order A, B, C, A (making A and C recently used); trigger eviction and verify B (least recently used) is removed. For LFU tests: add entries with known frequencies (A accessed 5 times, B accessed 1 time, C accessed 3 times); trigger eviction and verify B (least frequently used) is removed. Test tie-breaking (equal recency/frequency). Test that eviction actually frees cache space and allows new entries.
